rulings.Case Title: TIAN SHAN TRADING (USA), INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. NANCY HUANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 6, 2011, line 1, DEFENDANTS APPLETON YUM, CELLULAR GOPHER, LLC Motion For Monetary Sanctions. DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART. THE REQUEST FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS IS DENIED. HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF SHALL SERVE FURTHER RESPONSES TO THE DISCOVERY REQUESTS WITHOUT ANY AMENDMENTS TO THE REQUESTS AND WITHOUT OBJECTIONS WITHIN 20 DAYS. REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF SHALL PAY THE PAST DUE $940.00 IN SANCTIONS TO DEFENDANTS' PREVIOUSLY ORDERED, WITHIN 7 DAYS OF THIS HEARING DATE. PLAINTIFF SHALL PAY AN ADDITIONAL $640.00 IN SANCTIONS TO DEFENDANTS' WITHIN 15 DAYS. =(302/LMG)

THAT FILING IS THE OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AND IT IS IMPROPER TO SEEK AFFIRMATIVE RELIEF IN AN OPPOSTION. SUCH A REQUEST IS UNTIMELY PER CCP 1005. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT DAVID ROBISON VS. CITGO PETROLEUM CORPORATION, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Compel Defts Akzo Nobel Paints Llc, Akzo Nobel Coatings, Inc. And Masterchem Brands,Inc. To Produce Documents Responsive To Pltf'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, No. 4
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 6, 2011, line 1, PLAINTIFF SCOTT ROBISON'S Motion To Compel Defendants Akzo Nobel Paints LLC, Akzo Nobel Coatings, Inc. And Masterchem Brands,Inc. To Produce Documents Responsive To Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, No. 4. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL IS DENIED. THE PARTIES SHOULD WORK TOGETHER TO CREATE A STIPULATION/ORDER FOR PROTECTION OF THE CONFIDENTIALITY OF THE REQUESTED DOCUMENTS. IF THE PARTIES CANNOT STIPULATE TO TERMS IN THE PROTECTIVE ORDER, THE PARTIES MAY SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER FOR THE COURT'S CONSIDERATION. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JANESSA M JORDAN VS. DIRECTORY DISTRIBUTING ASSOCIATES, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Janessa Jordan Notice Of Motion And Unopposed Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 6, 2011, line 14, Plaintiff Janessa Jordan's Unopposed Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY APPROVAL OF CLASS ACTION SETTLEMENT IS DENIED IN PART. WHILE THE COURT IS WILLING TO APPROVE OF THE DEFINITION OF THE CLASS AND SETTLEMENT AMOUNT AND CALCULATIONS AS TO THE CLASS MEMBERS, IT DOES NOT APPROVE OF THE PERCENTAGE APPROACH TO THE CALCULATION OF ATTORNEY FEES. THEREFORE, THE COURT DIRECTS THE ATTORNEYS TO PROVIDE A LODESTAR ANALYSIS SO THE COURT CAN DETERMINE IF THE ATTORNEY FEE AMOUNT IS APPROPRIATE. THE ANALYSIS, FILED AS A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF, SHOULD BE PROVIDED TO THE COURT BY JULY 25TH. (COURTESY COPIES TO DEPARTMENT 302.) THE HEARING IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 1ST. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: MARIO GONZALES et al VS. INDYMAC MORTGAGE SERVICES, A DIVISION OF ONEWEST et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 6, 2011, line 11, DEFENDANT ONEWEST BANK, FSB, ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS INDYMAC DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER AS TO THE 1ST, 2ND, 3RD, 6TH, 7TH AND 8TH CAUSES OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THESE CAUSES OF ACTION ARISE OUT OF LOAN ORIGINATION, WHICH WAS UNDERTAKEN BY INDYMAC BANK AND NOT ONEWEST. ONEWEST DID NOT ASSUME LIABILITIES OF INDYMAC PER ITS AGREEMENT WITH THE FDIC; THEREFORE, THESE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE NOT PROPERLY ASSERTED AGAINST ONEWEST. AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE THAT PLAINTIFF DID ALL THAT THE CONTRACT REQUIRED THEM TO DO AND ALL CONDITIONS REQUIRED IN DEFENDANT'S PERFORMANCE HAD OCCURRED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO DO SO, AND IN FACT ALLEGES THAT THEY DEFAULTED UNDER THE TERMS OF THE CONTRACT. THERE IS NO POSSIBLE AMENDMENT THAT COULD CURE THIS DEFECT. AS TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: MARIO GONZALES et al VS. INDYMAC MORTGAGE SERVICES, A DIVISION OF ONEWEST et al

THE DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS IS GRANTED. THERE ARE NO REAL PROPERTY CLAIMS REMAINING. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY FEES IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S ACTED WITH SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION IN RECORDING THE LIS PENDENS. =(302/LMG)
